sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! The white at [9,0]-[63,0] is a horizontal bar at the top edge - that's the timer/progress bar! It went from 57px to 55px, meaning it's shrinking by 2 pixels per action. This is a HUD element, not gameplay.
The gameplay objects (white shape at [52,27]-[54,30], purple square with magenta, dark gray) didn't change at all. So clicking on the white shape at (53, 28) had no gameplay effect - it just consumed a step and the timer shrank.
I need to rethink my approach. Let me go back to basics and look at what's actually happening in the game.
Current state:
- Purple ring at top (12 pixels)
- Purple square (21px) at center (56, 27) containing magenta pixel
- White shape (6px) at (53, 28) - above purple square
- Dark gray shape (8px) at (60, 27) - below purple square
The magenta pixel is inside the purple square. The white and dark gray shapes are adjacent to it.
I've been clicking on the magenta pixel and it toggles the white/dark gray positions. I've also tried clicking on the dark gray and white shapes.
Maybe the goal is to get the dark gray shape to be adjacent to the purple ring? Or maybe I need to click on the purple ring?
Let me try clicking on the purple ring center at (21, 39) again.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated:
- White bar at top [9,0]-[63,0] is a HUD timer (shrinking: 57→55px)
- Gameplay objects unchanged after clicking white shape at (53,28)
- Magenta (M) inside purple square (p, 21px) at (56,27)
- White shape (W, 6px) at (53,28), Dark gray (G, 8px) at (60,27)
- Clicking magenta toggles white/dark gray positions
Plan: Try clicking on the purple ring center again to see if it triggers a new phase.
